summary.what: Fix countAutoSkipped to honor msg: rule
summary.why: v0.22.5 status undercounted the housekeeping-skipped tally when a msg: glob matched — the dedup in skipcount.go left countAutoSkipped on a parallel inline identity chain that was never updated for the new msg: rule. The user-visible symptom was 'timbers status' silently misreporting whether a freshly-added msg: rule was actually filtering anything. Caught in the deep review pass (timbers-00b) by both arch and code reviewers independently.
summary.how: Delegate identity classification to classifyByIdentity (the same chain filterByRules already uses) so visibility and the gate share one source of truth. Add msg: and author: regression cases to TestCountInfraSkippedSinceLatest, with the storage skipMessages/skipAuthors fields set directly on the test struct since the production loader reads .timbersignore from disk.

notes: Considered keeping countAutoSkipped as a separate loop for symmetry with the gate's filterByRules vs ExplainPending split, but rejected — the bug WAS that the loops drifted; making them re-converge is the fix, not a separation to preserve. Also considered adding a test that mutates the production code to confirm catch-coverage; skipped because both reviewers cross-validated the failure mode and the new test would have failed against the pre-fix code (verified mentally during writing).
